content:
- Processed PO email instruction set for pgBackRest task-direction changes + continue-ralph.
- Sent frequent PO updates (ack, plan, subtask start, subtask complete/move, blocker, final summary) via receive_mail/reply.sh.
- Located pgBackRest integration story tasks under `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/` and inserted a uniform PO directive in all 8 files: config-method ownership only; minimal CLI flags; avoid repo-local wrapper/hack patterns.
- Verified non-passing pgBackRest tasks remained non-passing after edits (05/06/07/08 kept `<passes>false</passes>`), explicitly ignoring already-passing tasks as requested.
- Executed continue-ralph skill flow: stopped ralph service, staged and sanitized index, committed (`auto-commit before ralph continue`), ran task switch.
- Encountered known blocker in `.ralph/ralph.sh` transient unit start (`Unit ralph-pgtuskmaster.service was already loaded or has a fragment file`); worked around by directly starting `systemctl --user start ralph-pgtuskmaster.service` and verified active status.
- Task switch produced finish email for task [361], and worker process is running (`agent-tawny-osprey.service` active).
- Next: wait for PO follow-up email. If asked to harden continue flow, likely patch `.ralph/ralph.sh` to handle already-loaded unit cleanly (but only on explicit request).

- Ran progress_read.
- Scanned `.ralph/current_tasks.md` for failing tasks (`<passes>false</passes>` / `<passing>false</passing>`).
- Found two failing bug tasks: `ha-action-deduping-suppresses-retry` and `unused-backup-recovery-mode-doc-configuration`.
- Selected `.ralph/tasks/bugs/ha-action-deduping-suppresses-retry.md` as next task because bug-first rule applies and it impacts core HA loop correctness (retry suppression risk) and is likely already fixed in code but needs verification/docs cleanup.
- Wrote chosen task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.

- Read prior progress via progress_read on startup as required.
- Received PO follow-up email asking: explain bug in full + investigate why dedupe exists.
- Followed email cadence rule: sent frequent updates (ack/start, understood+plan, validation complete, starting final write-up, final full summary).
- Did NOT change code because request was investigation/reporting only.
- Inspected `.ralph/tasks/bugs/ha-action-deduping-suppresses-retry.md`, `src/ha/decide.rs`, `src/ha/state.rs`, `src/ha/worker.rs`, `src/ha/actions.rs`, and HA contributor docs.
- Confirmed current behavior is already fixed vs original bug claim: dedupe is tick-local (`dedupe_actions_per_tick`), no `recent_action_ids` in HaState, retries across ticks covered by tests.
- Ran targeted tests:
  - cargo test --all-targets ha::decide::tests::actions_are_reissued_while_conditions_persist -- --exact (pass)
  - cargo test --all-targets ha::decide::tests::tick_local_dedupe_drops_duplicate_action_ids_preserving_order -- --exact (pass)
- Also confirmed worker-level regression `step_once_dispatches_start_postgres_every_tick_while_unreachable` exists and asserts repeated dispatch + distinct job ids.
- Sent PO full explanation: what the bug class is, operational impact, what current code does, why per-tick dedupe exists and why cross-tick dedupe is the wrong design.
- Next if PO asks: either create/adjust task to mark stale bug as resolved-by-existing-code or perform explicit task-file status changes, but only on direct instruction.

- Read prior progress log on startup as required.
- Received PO email asking for deeper justification: no code changes, no tests, only report.
- Followed email cadence heavily: sent immediate ack/start, plan-understanding update, subtask start, subtask complete, full detailed report, and final closure summary (6 emails total).
- Did direct investigation only (per workflow rule): inspected `src/ha/state.rs`, `src/ha/decide.rs`, `src/ha/actions.rs`, and `src/dcs/state.rs`.
- Extracted complete top-level HA phases (Init, WaitingPostgresReachable, WaitingDcsTrusted, Replica, CandidateLeader, Primary, Rewinding, Bootstrapping, Fencing, FailSafe).
- Extracted restore sub-state machine phases (`RestorePhase`) and their executor/non-executor guard behavior.
- Built full per-state condition -> actions -> next-state mapping from code branches.
- Key explanation delivered: decide is deterministic/pure per snapshot but intentionally can emit multiple actions per tick because it composes side effects; single-action-per-tick is not an invariant here.
- Clarified duplicate behavior: exact duplicate action IDs within one tick are deduped at end of decide (`dedupe_actions_per_tick`), while retries across ticks are still allowed.
- No code edits, no tests, no task creation; this was pure analysis/reporting and communication.
- Next if asked: provide compact machine-readable matrix (CSV/table) of transitions, or answer follow-up design objections.

- Read prior progress on startup.
- Received PO email with strict instruction: do not change code, do not run tests, answer design objections/questions.
- Followed heavy email cadence: sent immediate ack/start, plan-understanding email, subtask start email, subtask 1 completion email, subtask 2 start email, and final completion summary email.
- Did investigation-only pass across HA + process boundaries:
  - src/ha/decide.rs
  - src/ha/state.rs
  - src/ha/actions.rs
  - src/ha/worker.rs
  - src/process/state.rs
  - src/process/worker.rs
- Key conclusions I sent:
  - Starting Postgres before final role is currently true by design; flakiness concern is conceptually valid as policy-window concern.
  - Duplicate concurrent starts are not the core issue because process worker serializes jobs and rejects while busy.
  - Single composite-output action model is feasible (not impossible), but pushes complexity into sub-step failure/retry/observability semantics.
  - Bootstrap/rewind/fence already behave as long-running substates while process is Running (your PO model aligns there).
  - actionID in pure loop is a design choice for tick-local semantic dedupe, not a hard requirement.
  - PO concern about mixing long-running op state with HA policy is valid; suggested split-machine architecture as biggest simplification lever.
- No code edits made.
- No tests run.
- Next if asked: convert recommendations into concrete refactor task(s) using add-task-as-agent skill; still avoid direct implementation unless explicitly instructed.

- Read prior progress on startup.
- Received PO email asking for explanation-only of DCS cache behavior; strict no code changes and no tests.
- Followed required high-frequency email cadence via receive_mail/reply.sh: immediate ack, understanding+plan, subtask progress, prep-final, and completion summary.
- Investigated code paths:
  - src/dcs/state.rs (DcsState/DcsTrust/DcsCache model and evaluate_trust)
  - src/dcs/worker.rs (step_once behavior on write/drain/refresh errors; trust forcing)
  - src/dcs/store.rs (refresh_from_etcd_watch applies incremental put/delete/reset into cache)
  - src/dcs/etcd_store.rs (disconnect invalidation clears queue; reconnect bootstrap emits Reset+snapshot)
  - src/ha/decide.rs (hard gate: if trust != FullQuorum, enter FailSafe and avoid normal leader logic)
  - src/ha/worker.rs (SignalFailSafe/FollowLeader are coordination-only dispatch)
  - src/runtime/node.rs (startup probe may continue without cache)
- Conclusion prepared for user: cache exists because data source is watch-delta materialization, not full snapshot every loop; stale cache is preserved but explicitly untrusted via DcsTrust::NotTrusted and HA FailSafe gate.
- No code edits made.
- No tests run.
- Next if asked: provide a compact state-machine diagram for DCS trust transitions and HA gating in markdown.

- New PO email: "Show all dedupe possibilities" (investigation/report request, no code edits).
- Followed required high-frequency email cadence: immediate ack, plan, step progress (runtime done, validation done), final complete summary.
- Investigated dedupe points with ripgrep + file reads; no implementation work done.
- Confirmed production runtime dedupe locations:
  - src/ha/decide.rs per-tick action dedupe by ActionId.
  - src/dcs/worker.rs local member publish dedupe by pg snapshot version.
- Confirmed documented contract in docs/src/contributors/ha-pipeline.md: per-tick dedupe + no cross-tick suppression.
- Cataloged non-runtime dedupe/duplicate guards:
  - conninfo parser duplicate key rejection (test-only parser)
  - test harness config duplicate etcd member names
  - test harness port allocator seen-set retry
  - etcd3 harness member/port uniqueness checks
  - e2e metrics dedupe of committed keys and leader signatures
- Sent PO consolidated final inventory with exact file/line references and classification (runtime vs harness/test).
- No code edits, no tests run.

- New PO email demanded sharper answer: all situations in decide.rs that would produce duplicate actions inside a single decide call if dedupe were removed.
- Followed required email cadence: immediate ack, plan-understood update, subtask-start update, subtask-complete update, and final full report.
- Per request: no code edits and no test runs.
- Investigated src/ha/decide.rs and src/ha/actions.rs to map every candidate push site and ActionId equivalence semantics.
- Conclusion: no currently reachable runtime branch combination in decide.rs produces duplicate ActionId values within one decide invocation.
- Key reasoning I sent: top-level trust branches are exclusive; blocking restore suppresses normal phase logic; per-phase/per-restore-arm action pushes do not duplicate same ActionId in a single path; WriteRestoreStatus also does not double-push in reachable flow despite ActionId collapsing payload.
- Final report emailed in natural language with no bullet points, exactly as requested.

- Received PO email: stop Ralph systemd services; keep receive-mail running; confirm worker logs stop moving.
- Followed required email cadence with 5 updates: immediate ack, plan formed, subtask start, subtask complete/move to verification, and final completion summary.
- Inspected .ralph scripts (`ralph-status.sh`, `ralph-email-watcher.sh`) to identify service names and stop behavior.
- Stopped in-scope Ralph units: ralph-pgtuskmaster.service, ralph-event-watch.service, ralph-progress-watcher.path, ralph-pgtuskmaster-progress-watcher.path, ralph-progress-email.service, ralph-pgtuskmaster-progress-email.service, opencode-ralph.service.
- Left `receive-mail.service` active/running as explicitly requested.
- Confirmed shutdown via `systemctl --user` status + journal stability check: last `ralph-pgtuskmaster.service` line unchanged over 6 seconds (`log_tail_stable=yes`).
- No code changes were made.

- Received PO email task: create `review_branch` at repo first commit, push it, and switch back to master.
- Followed mandatory email cadence with 6 replies: immediate ack, plan formed, subtask start, subtask completion/move, completion update, and final summary.
- Found first commit via `git rev-list --max-parents=0 HEAD`: 861e9b3bc24bee1df2e285a449bb0739670789af.
- Created/updated local branch ref: `git branch -f review_branch <first-commit>`.
- Pushed branch: `git push origin review_branch --force` (new remote branch created).
- Switched back to `master` with `git switch master`.
- Verified refs: `review_branch` -> first commit, `master` remains at 5bc72f25f2698a50376604dfc987609f0a418f22.
- Noted dirty working tree contains pre-existing modified files; no code changes made by this task.

- Investigated `.ralph/ralph.sh` and related user systemd units to explain current behavior without changing anything.
- Confirmed `.ralph/ralph.sh` starts `ralph-pgtuskmaster` via `systemd-run --user --unit=ralph-pgtuskmaster --collect ...` and then tails its journal.
- Confirmed current live state: `ralph-pgtuskmaster.service` exists as a transient unit with fragment path `/run/user/501/systemd/transient/ralph-pgtuskmaster.service`, currently `loaded/inactive/dead`.
- Identified explanation for error `Unit ralph-pgtuskmaster.service was already loaded or has a fragment file`: script checks only `is-active`, so it still tries to create a fresh transient unit even when that unit name is already loaded but inactive.
- Mapped persistent user units that also exist here: `receive-mail.service`, `opencode-ralph.service`, `ralph-event-watch.service`, `ralph-pgtuskmaster-progress-watcher.path`, and matching progress-email services; current active one is `receive-mail.service`.
- Next if asked: explain the exact lifecycle tradeoff between transient worker units and persistent installed service files, or patch the startup logic if explicitly requested.

- User requested permanent removal of all Ralph-related systemd services, keeping only `receive-mail.service`.
- Inspected current user units and unit files first; identified installed files under `~/.config/systemd/user` for `opencode-ralph`, `ralph-event-watch`, both progress watcher/email pairs, and transient `ralph-pgtuskmaster.service`.
- Stopped/disabled/reset Ralph-related units, deleted all matching Ralph/opencode user unit files from `~/.config/systemd/user`, reloaded the user systemd manager, and removed the runtime transient fragment `/run/user/501/systemd/transient/ralph-pgtuskmaster.service`.
- Final verification:
  - `systemctl --user list-unit-files | rg '^(ralph|opencode-ralph|receive-mail)'` now shows only `receive-mail.service`.
  - `find ~/.config/systemd/user ...` now shows only `receive-mail.service`.
  - `receive-mail.service` remains active/running.
  - `ralph-pgtuskmaster.service` now shows as `not-found inactive dead`, meaning the unit file/fragment is gone and only a stale manager reference name remains visible in listing output.
- No repo code files were modified.
